basic_details.job_title: HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026: 27 Vacancies, Walk-In on 24 July
basic_details.post_name: ITI Trade Apprentice (One Year Apprenticeship Training Programme, 2026-27)
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Merit-based (70% weightage 10th grade + 30% weightage ITI marks); no written test or interview
basic_details.short_information: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), Barrackpore Division has released a notification (Advt. No. HR/TRG/2026/06/18, dated 9th July 2026) inviting eligible Indian citizens to apply for engagement as Trade Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The recruitment is for the One Year Apprenticeship Training Programme for the year 2026-27, with a total of 27 vacancies across five ITI trades. Selection is through a walk-in process, and there is no written test or interview.

application_fee.details: This information is not available in the official notification. No application fee details are mentioned for this recruitment. Candidates are advised to check for any updates on the official HAL website before the walk-in.

age_limit.details: Minimum age: 18 years. Maximum age: 26 years, as on 24/07/2026, for General category candidates. | Relaxation in the upper age limit is extended to SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates as per Government guidelines. The notification does not specify the exact number of years of relaxation for each category — candidates from reserved categories should confirm the applicable relaxation as per current Government of India norms.

qualification.details: Candidate should have passed regular and full-time 10th/HSC/SSC and ITI under the Craftsman Training Scheme (CTS) in the above trades, from a recognized institute only. Only candidates who passed their respective course in the year 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, or 2025 are eligible to apply. Candidates who have undergone, are undergoing, or are already registered under a NAPS Contract of Apprentice elsewhere are not eligible. Candidates with work experience of one or more years are not eligible. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

salary_details.details: The stipend shall be paid monthly as per the Apprentices Act 1961 and Rules and subsequent amendments. The exact stipend amount is not mentioned in the official notification. Candidates should refer to the applicable Apprentices Act stipend rates or contact HAL Barrackpore directly for the current figure.

selection_process: Selection is entirely merit-based. As per the notification:
70% weightage is given to marks scored in the 10th grade. 30% weightage is given to ITI marks. There will be no written test or interview.
The list of candidates shortlisted in the selection process will be hosted on the official HAL website
www.hal-india.co.in. Candidates are advised to keep checking the website regularly for updates.
how_to_apply: There is no online application for this recruitment. Eligible candidates must walk in directly as per the schedule below:
Training Auditorium Hindustan Aeronautics Limited, Barrackpore 9 Topkhana Road, Barrackpore North 24 Parganas, Kolkata-700120 Phone: 03325402035 / 2177
Register on the NAPS 2.0 portal and obtain a system-generated Registration Number.
Confirm you meet all eligibility criteria (qualification, passing year, age limit, no prior apprenticeship/NAPS registration elsewhere, no work experience of one year or more).
Prepare all original certificates and photocopies as listed in the documents section below.
Arrive at the Training Auditorium, HAL Barrackpore, by the reporting time of 10:00 Hrs on 24th July 2026.
Make your own arrangements for accommodation and travel, as the notification specifies HAL will not provide these.
important_instructions: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates must register on the NAPS 2.0 portal and obtain a system-generated Registration Number before the walk-in. Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility criteria before appearing for the walk-in, and that the particulars furnished at the time of document verification are true and correct in all respects. Submission of false or incorrect information, or suppression of facts, will lead to cancellation or termination of candidature at any stage, including during the training period. Only candidates who have passed the qualifying examination and possess Original/Provisional certificates as on 24.07.2026 are eligible. OBC candidates must produce a valid Non-Creamy Layer Certificate. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. HAL reserves the right to cancel, restrict, or modify the notification criteria and/or selection process without prior notice or reason. The number of vacancies can also be modified at the Company's discretion. HAL's decision on eligibility, acceptance/rejection of applications, and selection will be final and binding. After completion of the Apprenticeship Training, the apprenticeship contract will be terminated, and HAL, Barrackpore Division, is under no obligation to offer employment. Candidates must make their own arrangements for accommodation and travel for the walk-in process and for joining the apprenticeship.

content_sections.heading: Quick Overview: HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

rows.information: ITI Trade Apprentice (One Year Apprenticeship Training Programme, 2026-27)
Training Auditorium, HAL, Barrackpore, 9 Topkhana Road, North 24 Parganas, Kolkata-700120
Merit-based (70% weightage 10th grade + 30% weightage ITI marks); no written test or interview

content_sections.heading: About HAL Barrackpore
content_sections.text: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited is a Maharatna Central Public Sector Enterprise (CPSE) under the Ministry of Defence, Government of India. According to the notification, HAL is a premier aeronautical industry of South East Asia, operating 21 Production/Overhaul/Service Divisions and 10 collocated Research & Design (R&D) Centres, along with one Facility Management Division, spread across the country.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: The notification does not mention any separate online application window; eligible candidates are required to walk in directly with documents on the above date.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: As per the notification, candidates applying for any of the five trades above must have completed: Full-time 10th / HSC / SSC, and ITI under the Craftsman Training Scheme (CTS) in the respective trade (Welder, Turner, Machinist, Electrician, or Fitter), from a recognized institute only. The qualification requirement is the same across all five trades; there is no separate qualification listed per trade beyond this.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidate should have passed regular and full-time 10th/HSC/SSC and ITI under the Craftsman Training Scheme (CTS) in the above trades, from a recognized institute only. Only candidates who passed their respective course in the year 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, or 2025 are eligible to apply. Candidates who have undergone, are undergoing, or are already registered under a NAPS Contract of Apprentice elsewhere are not eligible. Candidates with work experience of one or more years are not eligible. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Minimum age: 18 years. Maximum age: 26 years, as on 24/07/2026, for General category candidates. Relaxation in the upper age limit is extended to SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates as per Government guidelines. The notification does not specify the exact number of years of relaxation for each category — candidates from reserved categories should confirm the applicable relaxation as per current Government of India norms.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: Reservations for engagement of Apprentices shall be applicable as per the Apprentices Act 1961 and Rules and Subsequent Amendments, and for wards of HAL employees as per HAL Corporate Office Guidelines. OBC candidates must produce a valid Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) Certificate. The notification does not specify a category-wise reserved seat breakdown.

content_sections.heading: Stipend / Salary
content_sections.text: The stipend shall be paid monthly as per the Apprentices Act 1961 and Rules and subsequent amendments. The exact stipend amount is not mentioned in the official notification. Candidates should refer to the applicable Apprentices Act stipend rates or contact HAL Barrackpore directly for the current figure.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: This information is not available in the official notification. No application fee details are mentioned for this recruitment. Candidates are advised to check for any updates on the official HAL website before the walk-in.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is entirely merit-based. As per the notification: 70% weightage is given to marks scored in the 10th grade. 30% weightage is given to ITI marks. There will be no written test or interview . The list of candidates shortlisted in the selection process will be hosted on the official HAL website, www.hal-india.co.in. Candidates are advised to keep checking the website regularly for updates.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply (Walk-In Process)
content_sections.text: There is no online application for this recruitment. Eligible candidates must walk in directly as per the schedule below: Training Auditorium Hindustan Aeronautics Limited, Barrackpore 9 Topkhana Road, Barrackpore North 24 Parganas, Kolkata-700120 Phone: 03325402035 / 2177 Register on the NAPS 2.0 portal and obtain a system-generated Registration Number. Confirm you meet all eligibility criteria (qualification, passing year, age limit, no prior apprenticeship/NAPS registration elsewhere, no work experience of one year or more). Prepare all original certificates and photocopies as listed in the documents section below. Arrive at the Training Auditorium, HAL Barrackpore, by the reporting time of 10:00 Hrs on 24th July 2026. Make your own arrangements for accommodation and travel, as the notification specifies HAL will not provide these.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Valid Aadhaar Card 10th / HSC / SSC Mark Sheet Birth Certificate (only if date of birth is not mentioned in the SSC/SSLC/10th Certificate) ITI NCVT Certificate (Original / Provisional) ITI Examination Marks Sheet(s) — all semesters or consolidated mark sheet Reservation / Community / Caste Certificate (SC, ST, OBC-NCL), if applicable One set of photocopy / Xerox copy of all the above certificates Passport-size photographs (3 copies) Details of Registration in the NAPS Portal

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates must register on the NAPS 2.0 portal and obtain a system-generated Registration Number before the walk-in. Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility criteria before appearing for the walk-in, and that the particulars furnished at the time of document verification are true and correct in all respects. Submission of false or incorrect information, or suppression of facts, will lead to cancellation or termination of candidature at any stage, including during the training period. Only candidates who have passed the qualifying examination and possess Original/Provisional certificates as on 24.07.2026 are eligible. OBC candidates must produce a valid Non-Creamy Layer Certificate. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. HAL reserves the right to cancel, restrict, or modify the notification criteria and/or selection process without prior notice or reason. The number of vacancies can also be modified at the Company's discretion. HAL's decision on eligibility, acceptance/rejection of applications, and selection will be final and binding. After completion of the Apprenticeship Training, the apprenticeship contract will be terminated, and HAL, Barrackpore Division, is under no obligation to offer employment. Candidates must make their own arrangements for accommodation and travel for the walk-in process and for joining the apprenticeship.

content_sections.heading: HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date or walk-in date for HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in date is 24th July 2026, with reporting time at 10:00 Hrs at the Training Auditorium, HAL Barrackpore.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 27 total vacancies across five trades: Welder (2), Turner (2), Machinist (7), Electrician (5), and Fitter (11).

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 26 years, as on 24/07/2026, for General category candidates. Age relaxation applies for SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates as per Government guidelines.

faq.question: Q4. Is there a written test or interview for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No. Selection is entirely merit-based, using 70% weightage of 10th grade marks and 30% weightage of ITI marks. There is no written test or interview.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for HAL Barrackpore ITI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: This information is not available in the official notification. No application fee is mentioned.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend for HAL Barrackpore ITI apprentices?
faq.answer: The stipend is paid monthly as per the Apprentices Act 1961 and Rules and subsequent amendments. The exact amount is not mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: Q7. Which candidates are eligible to apply?
faq.answer: Candidates who have passed regular, full-time 10th/HSC/SSC and ITI under the Craftsman Training Scheme (CTS) in the notified trades from a recognized institute, and who completed their course in 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, or 2025, are eligible. Candidates already registered under a NAPS apprenticeship elsewhere, or with one or more years of work experience, are not eligible.

faq.question: Q8. Is registration on the NAPS portal mandatory?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates must register on the NAPS 2.0 portal and obtain a system-generated Registration Number before appearing for the walk-in.

faq.question: Q9. Will HAL provide accommodation or travel allowance for the walk-in?
faq.answer: No. The notification states that candidates must make their own arrangements for accommodation and travelling, both for the walk-in process and for joining the apprenticeship.

faq.question: Q10. Does this apprenticeship guarantee a job at HAL after completion?
faq.answer: No. The notification clearly states that after completion of the Apprenticeship Training, the apprenticeship contract will be terminated, and there is no obligation on HAL, Barrackpore Division, to offer employment.
